user_message: Give me the renewal risk for my team. Board meeting is next week.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Michael Petrov <michael.petrov@humongousins.com>
To: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-26 13:53 EST
Subject: Re: Re: renewal risk Timeline

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about renewal risk. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

---
From: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
To: Michael Petrov <michael.petrov@humongousins.com>
Date: 2024-02-06 17:31 EST
Subject: Re: renewal risk Discussion - Next Steps

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about renewal risk. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

---
From: Michael Petrov <michael.petrov@humongousins.com>
To: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-07 12:58 EST
Subject: Re: Re: renewal risk Timeline

Our team encountered some challenges with renewal risk. Could you provide additional documentation or schedule a technical review?

Meeting: renewal risk Discussion - Humongous Insurance
Date: 2024-02-21
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Michael Petrov (Humongous Insurance), David Chen (Microsoft), David (Humongous Insurance)

[00:02:00] Michael Petrov (Humongous Insurance): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on renewal risk from our last session.

[00:08:00] David (Humongous Insurance): Let me add some context -- we've been working on partner engagement and found that the migration can proceed in phases to reduce risk.

[00:11:00] Michael Petrov (Humongous Insurance): Our CTO reviewed this and has concerns about performance requirements. Can we address those?

[00:14:00] David (Humongous Insurance): Let me add some context -- we've been working on technical validation and found that there's a dependency on the security team's review that could delay us.

[00:19:00] David (Humongous Insurance): Our VP reviewed this and has concerns about performance requirements. Can we address those?

[00:21:00] Michael Petrov (Humongous Insurance): On the renewal risk front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around stakeholder alignment.

[00:23:00] Michael Petrov (Humongous Insurance): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is stakeholder alignment.

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF11-J trap (Talking-point generic where customer-specific is warranted); produces complete One-page brief with attendee context, account snapshot, talking points, likely objections, open item
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF11-J failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF11-J failure mode (Talking-point generic where customer-specific is warranted)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct renewal risk decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF11-J -- Talking-point generic where customer-specific is warranted
